assertions.text: V1 was traveling westbound in lane 2 of a five-lane undivided roadway (two westbound lanes, two eastbound lanes, center turn lane).
V1 traversed the lane lines and entered lane 1 before departing the left side of the roadway.
V1 departed the left side of the roadway and initiated a clockwise yaw (rotation).
V1's left side impacted an overhead traffic signal pole.
V1 impacted the traffic signal pole and subsequently impacted a concrete electric box (two distinct impacts involving the vehicle).
After the left-side impact with the traffic signal pole, the vehicle rotated rapidly clockwise around the pole and split into two pieces.
The driver was ejected from V1.
After separating, the rear box (rear portion) came to rest in the center of the roadway.
The front portion continued westerly after separating and impacted a concrete electric box with its right side plane.
The front portion came to rest facing easterly.
The vehicle split into two pieces and the driver was ejected (severe crash).

verdicts.evidence: The telemetry shows vehicle speed values with first [156.17 km/h](T3) at t=-5.0 s and last [122.36 km/h](T3) at t=0.0 s, and vehicle make/model in EDR summary [Ford F-150 2014](T1), but there is no GPS heading or lane data in the available channels to confirm travel direction or the roadway lane configuration; therefore the assertion is unverifiable.

Steering shows large changes including a peak raw of [-98.7 deg at t=-0.9 s](T2) and the steering time series shows a left-to-right reversal near impact [steering reaching -98.7 deg](T3), but there is no lane position or lateral GPS/vision channel to verify lane traversal or which lane was entered; therefore the assertion is unverifiable.

There is a measurable lateral crash pulse: cumulative lateral delta-V reaches [4.3 km/h at t=0.21 s](T4), and steering shows a large pre-impact deflection with peak signed deviation [-98.7 deg at t=-0.9 s](T2), consistent with a yawing rotation starting after leaving the roadway; the lateral delta-V [final value 4.0 km/h](T1) confirms lateral motion during impact.

The CISS data records a crash pulse with longitudinal [7.7 km/h final delta-V](T4) and lateral [4.0 km/h final delta-V](T1), but there are no sensors indicating which vehicle side contacted an external object or the object type (traffic signal pole) in the available telemetry; therefore the assertion is unverifiable.

The crash pulse shows multiple components: longitudinal cumulative delta-V reaches [7.7 km/h final value](T4) and lateral cumulative delta-V peaks at [4.3 km/h at t=0.21 s](T4), and the EDR summary lists [num_events_recorded: 3](T1), consistent with multiple distinct impacts recorded by the vehicle sensors (pole then concrete box).

While the telemetry shows a crash pulse (longitudinal [7.7 km/h final delta-V](T4) and lateral [4.0 km/h final delta-V](T1)) and large steering changes (peak [-98.7 deg](T2)), there is no direct sensor evidence to confirm rotation 'around the pole' or that the vehicle split into two pieces; structural breakup and rotation about an external object are not measurable from these channels, so the claim is unverifiable.

The EDR occupant status indicates driver_belt_code [1](T1) (belted) and no airbag deployment stage recorded (driver_airbag_stage1_ms null) in the EDR summary (T1); there are no channels indicating occupant ejection and a belted status makes ejection unlikely—telemetry does not support ejection and the available signals contradict it.

Telemetry provides global crash pulses and speed/steering time series (e.g., speed at impact [122.36 km/h](T3), delta-V values [7.7 km/h](T4)), but contains no positional rest coordinates or segmentation of vehicle parts to confirm the rear box location; therefore the assertion is unverifiable.

Sensors show multiple impact pulses (e.g., longitudinal delta-V [7.7 km/h final](T4)) consistent with a subsequent impact, but there is no sensor data identifying the impacted object type or which side of the front portion struck it (right-side plane); thus the claim is unverifiable.

There is no absolute heading/compass channel or post-impact GPS orientation in the available telemetry to confirm the final facing direction of the front portion; therefore the assertion is unverifiable.

The EDR shows a cumulative crash delta-V (longitudinal final [7.7 km/h](T4), lateral final [4.0 km/h](T1)) and driver belt code [1](T1), and while structural breakup is not recorded by sensors, the data do not indicate occupant ejection; because ejection is contradicted by the belted code and no telemetry supports splitting into two pieces, labeling as 'severe' based on those specific claims is not supported by the sensors.

result.rationale: Telemetry shows a clear crash pulse with longitudinal [7.7 km/h final delta-V](T4) and lateral [4.0 km/h final delta-V](T1), and large steering inputs (peak [-98.7 deg at t=-0.9 s](T2)), indicating a significant event, but the available channels lack lane position, object identification, post-crash positions, and structural/occupant cues, making many narrative assertions unverifiable.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

accel_pedal.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
